preamble: The shift from the voiceless velar stop [k] to the high-back rounded vowel [u] is a form of vocalization. It typically occurs when the velar stop is in a 'weak' position, such as a syllable coda (before another consonant). The velar closure is lost, resulting in a labio-velar glide [w] which merges into the vowel space as [u].

examples.note: The velar stop in the cluster vocalized into a back rounded glide/vowel.
The labiovelar weakened into a pure vowel.
